clinical_history: ECG recorded from a 54 year-old man. The findings are most consistent with which ONE of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: The ECG shows sinus rhythm with a PR of about 120 ms and no delta waves. The key findings are pathologic Q waves inferiorly with ischemic ST elevations in II, III and aVF (and reciprocal ST depressions in aVL), consistent with an evolving ST elevation-Q wave MI. The tall right precordial R waves with ST depressions and borderline prominent Q waves in V6 with slight ST elevations are consistent with concomitant postero-lateral involvement. The patient had advanced coronary atherosclerosis with a complete mid-occlusion of a dominant right coronary artery. Additional leads V7-V9 would likely show Q waves and ST elevations.  RVH would not account for inferior Q waves and ST changes. Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy can cause tall right precordial R waves and infero-lateral Q waves, but not the ST-T changes seen here.
